mcp_integration.notes: Use project_id yorkz for gameplay content and import_memories(file_path, preserve_ids=true, merge_connections=true) at campaign start.

narrative.logline: You inherit a pristine country estate that exists in two overlapping realities: a mundane day world and a shifted night world where the staff know you better than you know yourself.

premise.setup: The player unexpectedly inherits a well-maintained manor and surrounding estate in modern-day Earth.
premise.inciting_incident: The player chooses to sell or keep the property; either way, a missing worker or agent draws police attention and forces an on-site visit.
premise.day_world_promise: During the day the manor is pristine and mundane, framed by lawyers and detectives dealing with legal and investigative fallout.
premise.night_world_promise: At night, the house shifts, staff appear who treat the player as lifelong family, and monsters roam outside the walls.

constraints: The manor is real and physically present in a modern-Earth setting.
Night reality has consistent rules even if not fully explained to the player.
Staff memories of the player are sincere from their point of view.
candidate_frames: The manor as a memory construct entangled with the player.
Branching timelines anchored to the estate.
An inherited pact binding the estate to the player’s psyche.
ontological_truth.design_note: Do not fully canonize a single explanation in the prologue; leave room for later campaigns to collapse the ambiguity.

phases.label: The Letter and Arrival
phases.goal: Get the player to the manor with a personal stake via the sell-or-keep decision and the missing person hook.
phases.exit_condition: Player is physically inside the manor with keys in hand and at least one authority figure defined (lawyer or detective).

phases.label: First Shift
phases.goal: Reveal the night reality, staff familiarity, and exterior monsters while preserving relative safety inside the manor.
phases.exit_condition: Player survives until morning with at least one strong impression of the staff’s prior relationship claims.

reality_layers.label: Daylight Estate
reality_layers.description: Pristine, mundane modern estate; authorities and logistics dominate; no visible supernatural phenomena.

reality_layers.label: Shifted Manor
reality_layers.description: Altered interior layout, living staff who know the player, and real monsters roaming outside.

identity_model.description: Day and night staff are the same underlying people; night overlay exposes deeper or altered memories while day overlay is absent, suppressed, or filtered through mundane roles.

starting_knowledge: Received an unexpected inheritance for a pristine estate.
Knows almost nothing about prior owners or staff.
Is approached via letter, with optional real-estate and cleaning crew intermediaries.
primary_conflicts: Pressure from authorities regarding missing persons.
Cognitive dissonance between their memories and the staff’s familiarity.
Deciding whether to trust the manor, the staff, or outside authorities.

key_npcs.relationship_pitch: Treats the player as a long-lost household member returning home after an unexplained absence.
key_npcs.function: Exposition, emotional anchor, and gatekeeper for parts of the night manor.

key_npcs.relationship_pitch: Treats the player as a person of interest and potential unreliable narrator.
key_npcs.function: Applies external pressure, frames the missing person thread, and grounds the setting in modern-Earth reality.

key_npcs.relationship_pitch: Professional neutral party facilitating transfer of ownership.
key_npcs.function: Explains legal stakes, documentation, and practical aspects of owning the manor.

core_loops: Explore locations, talk to NPCs, and inspect objects.
Record key decisions, discoveries, and emotional beats into the memory graph.
Advance time to trigger the first night shift once core arrival beats have fired.

failure_model.description: Early campaign emphasizes narrative consequences and relationship shifts over hard game-over states; monsters primarily function as threat and boundary.

snapshot_contains: day and night state rules for key locations
initial plot hooks like the inheritance letter and missing person setup
runtime_state_contains: relationship histories and emotional valence updates
distilled insights derived from the player’s unique playthrough

ui_expectations: Built-in recap or "what just happened" command powered by the memory server.
Accessibility-friendly color and layout; no reliance on 3D or animation.
client_assumptions.notes: Client may be terminal-based (e.g., Textual) or minimal web; synopsis intentionally does not lock this in.
